It might be helpful to consider if these thoughts are always true, or if they are just one possible interpretation of the situation. What evidence supports these thoughts? And what evidence contradicts them?

patient.query: I guess when she yells, I just feel really small, like I can't ever meet her expectations. It’s like this wave of shame washes over me, and I think, “Here I go again, messing everything up.” But I don’t know… sometimes I’ve done things right. I just can’t seem to hold onto that when things get tense. It’s hard to think clearly in those moments.

automatic_thoughts: I can't ever meet her expectations
Here I go again, messing everything up

entailment: Tends to turn anger against self (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

In these situations, it's understandable to feel overwhelmed and like you might not meet her expectations. It's important to remember that everyone makes mistakes sometimes, and it doesn't define your worth. Let's work together on finding ways to manage your emotions when things get tense so you can think more clearly.
